name: Kaohsiung (Taiwan)

colors.symbolism: the harbour, the sea, and Kaohsiung's identity as Taiwan's principal port
purity, peace and a bright future

description: A blue field charged at the centre with a stylised white-and-yellow emblem combining a maritime motif (waves and harbour) with abstract letterforms.
history: Kaohsiung, southern Taiwan's largest city and the country's principal port, was promoted to a special municipality in 1979 and was merged with the surrounding Kaohsiung County on 25 December 2010 to form the present enlarged municipality. The current city flag dates from the 2010 merger; earlier versions of the flag, dating to 1981, retained a similar maritime symbolism.

features.symbolism: the harbour and southern sea that define the city
stylised maritime motif of waves, harbour and abstract letterforms, expressing Kaohsiung's role as Taiwan's principal port and southern industrial centre
